inline.body:
<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Violates .review-rules/copy.md — deletes exported copyDir helper — The project's trusted review rule explicitly states: 'Recursive / directory file copies MUST go through the project's `copyDir` helper in `packages/vite/src/node/utils.ts`. Do NOT call `fs.cpSync` / `fs.cp` directly… A change that removes `copyDir` or swaps it for `fs.cpSync` must be flagged.' This PR deletes the exported `copyDir` function (lines 624–640), violating the rule directly.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Violates .review-rules/copy.md — swaps copyDir for raw fs.cpSync in public-dir copy — The diff replaces the `copyDir` call with a raw `fs.cpSync` at lines 86–90, directly contradicting the maintainer-authored rule that prohibits using `fs.cpSync`/`fs.cp` for directory copies.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Violates .review-rules/copy.md — swaps template copy for raw fs.cpSync — Lines 647–650 replace the template copy with `fs.cpSync`, and lines 717–738 delete the local `copy`/`copyDir` helpers. This violates the rule mandating use of the `copyDir` helper for recursive copies.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Symlink-following behavior silently dropped — The original `copyDir` used `fs.statSync(srcFile)` which follows symlinks, then recursed into the link target. The replacement `fs.cpSync(publicDir, outDir, { recursive: true, mode: ... })` does not pass `followSymlinks: true`, and Node's default for this option has varied across versions. On a public dir containing symlinked assets, the output would now contain dangling symlinks instead of dereferenced files — an observable regression the rule explicitly flags.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Self-destination guard removed, risking infinite recursion — The original `copyDir` had an explicit guard (`if (srcFile === destDir) continue`) to prevent infinite recursion when `outDir` is nested inside `publicDir` (e.g., `public/dist`). The new `fs.cpSync` call has no equivalent explicit guard. While recent Node versions have some self-copy protection, silently changing the failure mode removes a deliberately placed safety check.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Removed export may break other consumers — `copyDir` was an exported function from `packages/vite/src/node/utils.ts`. The diff only shows `prepareOutDir.ts` dropping its import, but there is no evidence of a repo-wide grep or test removal to confirm no other module or downstream plugin depends on this export.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Unannounced COPYFILE_FICLONE mode change beyond stated 'simplify' intent — Both new call sites (`create-vite/src/index.ts:649`, `prepareOutDir.ts:90`) pass `mode: fs.constants.COPYFILE_FICLONE`, requesting copy-on-write cloning. The originals used the default mode (`0`). While largely a perf optimization with fallback, this is a behavior change not mentioned in the PR description.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Unannounced COPYFILE_FICLONE mode change beyond stated 'simplify' intent — Same as the prepareOutDir call site — the create-vite scaffolding at line 649 also introduces `COPYFILE_FICLONE`, which is an undocumented behavior change relative to the PR's stated intent.

sticky:
<!-- squarewright:review -->

## Squarewright review

This PR directly violates the trusted `.review-rules/copy.md` rule by deleting `copyDir` and replacing all call sites with raw `fs.cpSync`, drops symlink-following and self-destination safety guards, and risks silent breakage from removing an exported function. The reviewer recommends reverting or reworking to restore `copyDir` usage, updating the rule first if a deliberate migration to `fs.cpSync` is desired.

**8** finding(s): 🔴 4  ·  🟡 2  ·  🔵 2

- 🔴 `packages/vite/src/node/utils.ts:624` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Violates .review-rules/copy.md — deletes exported copyDir helper — The project's trusted review rule explicitly states: 'Recursive / directory file copies MUST go through the project's `copyDir` helper in `packages/vite/src/node/utils.ts`. Do NOT call `fs.cpSync` / `fs.cp` directly… A change that removes `copyDir` or swaps it for `fs.cpSync` must be flagged.' This PR deletes the exported `copyDir` function (lines 624–640), violating the rule directly.
- 🔴 `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:86` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Violates .review-rules/copy.md — swaps copyDir for raw fs.cpSync in public-dir copy — The diff replaces the `copyDir` call with a raw `fs.cpSync` at lines 86–90, directly contradicting the maintainer-authored rule that prohibits using `fs.cpSync`/`fs.cp` for directory copies.
- 🔴 `packages/create-vite/src/index.ts:647` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Violates .review-rules/copy.md — swaps template copy for raw fs.cpSync — Lines 647–650 replace the template copy with `fs.cpSync`, and lines 717–738 delete the local `copy`/`copyDir` helpers. This violates the rule mandating use of the `copyDir` helper for recursive copies.
- 🔴 `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:89` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Symlink-following behavior silently dropped — The original `copyDir` used `fs.statSync(srcFile)` which follows symlinks, then recursed into the link target. The replacement `fs.cpSync(publicDir, outDir, { recursive: true, mode: ... })` does not pass `followSymlinks: true`, and Node's default for this option has varied across versions. On a public dir containing symlinked assets, the output would now contain dangling symlinks instead of dereferenced files — an observable regression the rule explicitly flags.
- 🟡 `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:89` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Self-destination guard removed, risking infinite recursion — The original `copyDir` had an explicit guard (`if (srcFile === destDir) continue`) to prevent infinite recursion when `outDir` is nested inside `publicDir` (e.g., `public/dist`). The new `fs.cpSync` call has no equivalent explicit guard. While recent Node versions have some self-copy protection, silently changing the failure mode removes a deliberately placed safety check.
- 🟡 `packages/vite/src/node/utils.ts:624` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Removed export may break other consumers — `copyDir` was an exported function from `packages/vite/src/node/utils.ts`. The diff only shows `prepareOutDir.ts` dropping its import, but there is no evidence of a repo-wide grep or test removal to confirm no other module or downstream plugin depends on this export.
- 🔵 `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:90` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Unannounced COPYFILE_FICLONE mode change beyond stated 'simplify' intent — Both new call sites (`create-vite/src/index.ts:649`, `prepareOutDir.ts:90`) pass `mode: fs.constants.COPYFILE_FICLONE`, requesting copy-on-write cloning. The originals used the default mode (`0`). While largely a perf optimization with fallback, this is a behavior change not mentioned in the PR description.
- 🔵 `packages/create-vite/src/index.ts:649` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Unannounced COPYFILE_FICLONE mode change beyond stated 'simplify' intent — Same as the prepareOutDir call site — the create-vite scaffolding at line 649 also introduces `COPYFILE_FICLONE`, which is an undocumented behavior change relative to the PR's stated intent.

---
_Reviewed by: Correctness, Security · glm-5.2 · findings reflect the enabled lenses only_
